page: Microsoft Azure D2plds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Dpldsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs powered by a Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor operating at 3.4 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. For storage, the server is equipped with 118 GB of local disk capacity and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. It does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates average single-core performance, highlighted by a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 2074.19 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1671.0. Single-threaded gzip compression is a notable strength, registering a strong throughput of 33.09 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory-bound performance metrics are generally weak or poor. The Geekbench multi-core score is 3077.0, and the Passmark CPU score sits in the bottom 10% at 1981.81. Memory performance is similarly constrained, with a Passmark memory score of 1091.3 and a weak uncached write bandwidth of 21.27 GB/sec.

The D2plds_v6 offers a qualitative cost-efficient entry point for ARM64 architecture testing and lightweight deployments where dedicated CPU resources are required without the overhead of larger memory footprints. Due to the low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2 GB per core and weak multi-threaded scaling, it is not suited for memory-intensive databases, heavy multi-threaded compilation, or large-scale LLM inference. Appropriate workloads include single-threaded utility scripts, lightweight static web serving, basic edge processing, and development or testing environments optimized for ARM64 instruction sets.
description: Microsoft Azure D2plds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs on a 3.4 GHz Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor. It is configured with 4.0 GB of RAM, 118 GB of local storage, and supports Premium Storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance provides a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, positioning it as a low-memory, compute-focused option. Benchmark results indicate average single-core capabilities, with a strong single-threaded gzip compression speed of 33.09 GB/sec, though multi-core and memory performance metrics fall into the weak to poor ranges. Qualitatively, the server offers a cost-effective platform for dedicated ARM64 processing. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks, static web serving, and ARM development or testing environments rather than memory-intensive or highly parallelized workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2plds_v6 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated Neoverse-N2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 118 GB of local storage, optimized for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2plds_v6 is an ARM64 virtual machine with 2 dedicated vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and 118 GB local storage for lightweight workloads.
tagline: A dedicated ARM64 virtual machine with local storage, optimized for lightweight single-threaded processing and cost-effective development environments.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs.
Equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory and 118 GB of local storage.
Delivers strong single-threaded gzip compression performance of 33.09 GB/sec.
Exhibits weak multi-core and memory bandwidth performance in benchmark testing.
Well-suited for lightweight static web serving, ARM64 testing, and single-threaded utility tasks.
